name: Polenta with Tomatoes & Olives
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1-3/4 pints salted water to a boil.
2. Reduce the heat to low and add the polenta as you would for porridge. Stir continuously to prevent lumps.
3. Cook over the gentlest possible heat for about 20 minutes, stirring more or less continuously until the mixture is smooth and thick enough that your wrist aches from stirring.
4. Remove from heat, stir in 1 tablespoon olive oil, stoned olives (if using), and salt and pepper to taste.
5. Grease an 11-12 inch frying pan with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
6. Transfer the polenta into the pan, pack it down smoothly, and level the top with an oiled spoon.
7. Set aside for a couple of hours until the polenta is cold and solid. Loosen it with a palette knife, turn out of the pan, and cut into 6-8 wedges.
8. To make the sauce, finely chop the onion and sweat in olive oil for 10-12 minutes.
9. Add roughly chopped tomatoes and their juices, several cloves of finely chopped garlic, and rosemary, bay leaves, thyme (or other herbs).
10. Let the mixture bubble gently for about 40 minutes, stirring occasionally until reduced to a rich, fragrant sauce.
11. Remove herbs, season with salt and pepper, and add extra fresh herbs if desired.
12. Fry polenta wedges in very hot olive oil or unsalted butter (or combination) for 4-5 minutes on each side until lightly crusted and heated through.
13. Serve piping hot with the garlicky tomato sauce and olives or grated Parmesan, if desired. Polenta can also accompany small fried fish, sauteed chicken livers, grilled sausages, or be served alone.
